LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN RAMIREZ'S MOTION TO APPROVE PAGA SETTLEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO APPROVE PAGA SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT THE AMOUNT OF THE GROSS SETTLEMENT PAYMENT ALLOCATED TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S ATTORNEYS' FEES IS REDUCED TO $75,000, AND THE $12,500 ALLOCATED TO PAGA PAYMENTS IS INCREASED TO $25,000 (OF WHICH THE LWDA SHALL RECEIVE 75% OR $18,750 AND AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES SHALL RECEIVE 25% OR $6,250). ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Paga Settlement; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN RAMIREZ'S Motion To Approve Paga Settlement.

Plaintiff's motion to approve PAGA settlement is granted on the condition that the amount of the gross settlement payment allocated to plaintiff's counsel's attorneys' fees is reduced to $75,000, and the $12,500 allocated to PAGA payments is increased to $25,000 (of which the LWDA shall receive 75% or $18,750 and aggrieved employees shall receive 25% or $6,250).

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notic Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JEH ENTERPRISES, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is Off Calendar. Duplicate entry.

Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kyle Nordrehaug, esq. (858-551-1223 x.1002) of Blumenthal, Nordrehaug & Bhowmik for Plaintiff via Court Call.
Roman Zhuk, esq. (650-712-8908) of Fine, Boggs & Perkins LLP for Defendant via Court Call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JEH ENTERPRISES, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Defendant JEH Enterprises, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is denied. Plaintiff Christian Ramirez's complaint is a representative action containing a single cause of action in which Plaintiff is acting as a private attorney general and is seeking solely civil penalties. As such, under controlling law, Plaintiff's PAGA claims are not subject to arbitration or waiver pursuant to a predispute arbitration agreement. (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348, 382-383; Betancourt v. Prudential Overall Supply (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 439, 445-446; Esparza v. KS Industries, L.P. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1228, 1247 ["if Employee intends to (1) limit the claims pursued to PAGA representative claims seeking civil penalties paid largely into state coffers and (2) waive the claims for individualized relief (such as the recovery of unpaid wages pursuant to Lab. Code, 558), then the litigation can proceed on those limited claims."].) Unlike the plaintiff in Esparza, Plaintiff here has "unambiguously state[d] his intention" not to pursue private claims for victim-specific relief, such as claims to recover wages under Labor Code section 558. (Esparza, 13 Cal.App.5th at 1234.) As Defendant concedes, it disagrees with the California Supreme Court's decision in Iskanian, which is binding on this Court. (Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court (1962) 57 Cal.2d 450, 455.)

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
